title: althaea danubialis
intro: Althaea danubialis, also known as the Danube marshmallow, is a perennial plant species in the mallow family native to Europe. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its mucilaginous roots, which are rich in polysaccharides that have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. The health benefits of Althaea danubialis include relief from digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for coughs and sore throats. Althaea danubialis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including dried roots, teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed or applied topically to alleviate symptoms.
